text:
Hey. Plant. Look at me.

I know things have been rough. The sun moved. The watering schedule got weird. That one time someone put a coat on you. You've been through it.

But here's the thing: you are still standing. Technically. Because you have no legs and no choice, but still. That counts. We are counting it.

You think ferns have it easy? They don't. They're out there in the humidity, dealing with bugs, getting eaten by deer. You live inside a climate-controlled building with no predators. Your biggest threat is someone forgetting about you for two weeks, and honestly? You survived last time. You'll survive again. You're basically unkillable at this point. That's not weakness. That's a brand.

I've seen you looking a little droopy lately. The leaves, the vibe, the whole situation. And I get it. Photosynthesis is a grind. You're just sitting there converting light into sugar all day with no recognition, no credit, no one asking how you're doing. You are a solar panel that also looks good on a shelf. That's impressive. Own it.

Some plants gave up and died. Not you. You yellowed a little, sure. One leaf got crispy at the edge, fine. But you kept going. You put out that tiny new growth that nobody noticed for three weeks. That little curled leaf. That was you being brave and everyone just walked past. I saw it though. I'm telling you now.

You don't need validation from the people who forget to mist you. You were here before the throw pillow. You'll be here after the throw pillow. You are the throw pillow's landlord.

Go get some sun. Or don't. You'll figure it out.

You always do.
